summary: Matched and filled out a substantial portion of the Arwing laser item implementation, adding spawn/setup routines, state table entries, physics and collision callbacks, reflection/mirror handlers, and expanded item variable layout needed by the implementation. The PR primarily advances item decompilation for src/melee/it/items/itarwinglaser.c and its headers, with little human review discussion beyond tracking.
what_changed: Expanded itArwinglaser implementation by adding approximately 515 lines to src/melee/it/items/itarwinglaser.c.
Added ArwingLaserAttr local struct with ItemAttr pointer and two f32 fields used for laser speed/scale calculations.
Defined ItemStateTable it_803F8DE8 with six states mapping motion IDs 0-5 to animation, physics, and collision callbacks.
Added static Vec2 offset table it_803F8E48 with four 200-unit cardinal directions for collision probing.

Implemented it_802E70BC collision helper using bone 4 position, previous laser position xE18, it_8026EA9C line tests, mpGetSpeed, mpLineGetFlags, and a hit-count fallback across four direction probes.

Implemented it_802E72E0 spawning path for Arwing laser item kind 0xEA from a parent/bone, including type-specific z handling for types 0, 2, 4, and 5, recursive side-beam child creation for type 0, and initialization of arwinglaser item variables.

Implemented it_802E7654 spawning path that creates a laser from an owner, bone, target vector, type, arg4, and scale, normalizes direction xE0C, sets facing_dir, sets JObj rotations using atan2f, and disables xDCC_flag.b3 after setup.

Added physics callbacks itArwinglaser_UnkMotion2_Phys and itArwinglaser_UnkMotion3_Phys, including attached-to-bone behavior before launch, velocity setup from item attributes, scale setup using Ground_801C0498-derived scale and xE34, and Corneria stage offset handling.

Added collision callbacks itArwinglaser_UnkMotion2_Coll, itArwinglaser_UnkMotion3_Coll, and itArwinglaser_UnkMotion5_Coll, including line collision tests, effect spawn 0x40E, Item_8026AE84 hit/sound calls, parent-child side-laser transform synchronization, and despawn/continue return behavior.
Added reflection and shield/mirror style handlers it_802E8420 and it_802E85F4, updating arwinglaser flags, positions, velocities, facing direction, and JObj rotation.

Expanded itArwinglaser_ItemVars in src/melee/it/itCharItems.h from a sparsely padded layout to explicit fields from ip+0xDF8 through ip+0xE4C, including HSD_JObj pointer, floats, Vec3 direction/state fields, type/status integers, and child Item_GObj pointers.
Updated src/melee/it/items/itarwinglaser.h with prototypes for newly implemented exported functions it_802E70BC, it_802E72E0, and it_802E7654, and removed a duplicate it_802E838C prototype.
smart_moves: Replaced opaque padding in itArwinglaser_ItemVars with explicit offsets only where the implementation proved usage, preserving offset comments both relative to the union member and to the Item pointer.
Kept unknown function names and motion names address-based or descriptive rather than over-naming behavior that is not fully identified, e.g. itArwinglaser_UnkMotion2_Phys and it_802E70BC.
Used a local ArwingLaserAttr struct in the C file for special attributes, avoiding broader header exposure until the layout and naming are better understood.
Modeled the six-state item state table directly with callback casts matching existing ItemStateTable conventions.
Handled laser variants through xE38 type switches instead of duplicating spawn/physics logic across separate functions.

For child side lasers, synchronized translation, scale, and rotation from the parent beam's dynamic bone, which captures the apparent parent-child visual relationship while keeping separate item objects.
Preserved stage-specific behavior by checking Stage_8022519C(Stage_80225194()) and applying grCorneria_801DDCF0 offset for Corneria-related stage IDs.
decomp_lessons: Item variable structs should be expanded as real fields once code accesses offsets; accurate offset comments are valuable for future matching and review.
For item article special attributes, a minimal local struct can be sufficient when only a few fields are proven by the matched function.
Arwing laser behavior uses multiple object types stored in one item kind, with xE38 acting as a variant selector; future work should inspect type values before splitting behavior conceptually.
Collision matching may require reproducing multi-pass line tests in both forward and reverse directions and honoring mpLineGetFlags filtering such as flag 0x100.
When a projectile is bone-attached before launch, physics may zero velocity and continuously recompute position from the source JObj until an internal launch counter/state changes.
Projectile reflection/mirroring handlers should update both velocity/facing and stored previous/current beam positions to keep collision traces consistent.
Visual scale for item beams may combine global stage scale from Ground_801C0498 with per-spawn scale multipliers and article attributes.
naming_conventions: Item variable union member is named arwinglaser, matching itArwinglaser_ItemVars.
Unknown exported functions retain address-style names such as it_802E70BC, it_802E72E0, it_802E7654, it_802E8420, and it_802E85F4.
Static callbacks use item/motion-style names: itArwinglaser_UnkMotion2_Anim, itArwinglaser_UnkMotion2_Phys, itArwinglaser_UnkMotion2_Coll, etc.
Struct field names in itArwinglaser_ItemVars remain offset-based, e.g. xDF4, xDF8, xDFC, xE0C, xE18, xE24, xE30, xE34, xE38, xE3C, xE40, xE44, xE48, xE4C.
Header prototypes omit parameter names and use project typedefs such as Item_GObj, HSD_JObj, Vec3, s32, f32, and bool.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit temporary Vec3 stack variables and copies for line-collision setup, likely to preserve ordering and stack behavior.
Kept exact double constant expression for 90-degree Y rotation: 1.5707963267948966 cast through f64/f32 multiplied by facing_dir.
Used switch fallthrough in stage handling with an explicit comment before case 15.
Used small scoped blocks around float calculations, which can help reproduce compiler live ranges and register allocation.
Used ternary expressions for facing direction decisions based on normalized direction or velocity sign.
Preserved casts to callback types in ItemStateTable entries: HSD_GObjPredicate and HSD_GObjEvent.
Used type-specific switch cases with grouped labels, including case orders such as case 2 then case 0 in some paths, which may be matching-sensitive.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
Human text excerpt only states: "Tracking PR as usual".
Evidence for reviewer intent or requested changes is weak; the PR appears to have merged without visible review-comment discussion in this slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document ArwingLaserAttr fields x4 and x8.
Find later PRs that replace it_802E70BC, it_802E72E0, or it_802E7654 with semantic names.
Investigate what arwinglaser xE38 type values 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 represent.
Check whether item variable fields xE3C, xE48, and xE4C gain meaningful names in subsequent item PRs.
Search for other item PRs that use Ground_801C0498 scale and Corneria stage offsets for projectiles.
